text.title: PostTool Hook Self-Correction Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every PostToolUse self-correction hook MUST enforce.

rule.statement: Each validator command MUST be scoped to the affected file via $CLAUDE_FILE_PATH (or equivalent), NOT run against the whole repo.
rule.rationale: Repo-wide validation on every Edit explodes latency; the agent never finishes a turn before the next hook fires.

rule.statement: Validator commands MUST exit non-zero on failure so stderr is surfaced to the agent as a tool-result error.
rule.rationale: stderr from non-zero exits is the channel that wires self-correction. Silent success blocks the feedback loop.

rule.statement: Every hook command MUST carry a timeout (default 5s; max 30s). Long validators are deferred to commit-time gates.
rule.rationale: Hooks block the next turn; runaway commands stall the agent visibly.

rule.statement: The hook system MUST cap consecutive self-correction loops at retry_max (default 3). After retry_max failures on the same file, the hook MUST surface a structured "stuck" message instead of looping.
rule.rationale: Infinite self-correction on a structurally broken file (missing dependency, syntax beyond fix range) drains budget.

rule.statement: Any file pattern exempted from hooks (e.g., migrations/*.py) MUST be listed in decision.exempt_globs and documented inline.
rule.rationale: Silently exempting paths creates surprise; reviewers must see the carveouts.

rule.statement: When language == 'python' AND runtime_per_file < 5s, the spec MUST use ruff (check + format) as the validator.
rule.rationale: ruff replaces black + isort + flake8 + many pylint rules with one fast Rust binary.

rule.statement: When language == 'typescript' AND runtime_per_file < 5s, the spec MUST chain tsc --noEmit AND eslint --max-warnings 0 per file.
rule.rationale: tsc catches type errors; eslint catches style + correctness lints. Both are required for typescript projects.

rule.statement: When runtime_per_file >= 5s, the spec MUST defer the validator to a commit-time gate (pre-commit hook) rather than PostToolUse.
rule.rationale: Sub-second hooks are fine; multi-second hooks stall the agent and waste tokens on lint waits.
